name: Defra Local Authority Treescapes Fund
eligibility_notes: England-only. Local authorities and partnerships. Targets non-woodland trees outside woodlands — strong fit for street tree planting in urban LSOAs.

name: Mayor of London — Greener City Fund (Grow Back Greener)
eligibility_notes: Greater London only. Community groups, schools, councils. Smaller scheme but fast turnaround; good for community-facing portions of intervention package.

name: Active Travel England — Local Transport Capability Fund
eligibility_notes: Repackaging opportunity: cool-pavement and shade interventions can qualify if framed as pedestrian-comfort improvements alongside walking-route enhancement. Larger ceiling than single-purpose cooling funds.

name: National Lottery Heritage Fund — Green Spaces for Health
eligibility_notes: UK-wide. Strong fit when intervention has a community-health framing or sits in a heritage context (e.g. Victorian terraced streets).

name: GLA Urban Cooling Pilot Grant
eligibility_notes: London boroughs only. Pilot — not always open. Small ceiling but fast-tracked, and explicitly funds cool-pavement / cool-roof works that don't fit elsewhere.

name: DLUHC UK Shared Prosperity Fund — Local
eligibility_notes: Allocated to local authorities. Wide scope. Repackage public-realm cooling work as place-based regeneration to access the larger ceiling.

name: DfE — Climate Change Strategy: School Capital
eligibility_notes: Schools only. Useful when an LSOA has a school site that overlaps with the intervention zone — playground depaving and shade structures qualify.

name: Trees for Cities — Edible Playgrounds & Tree-Planting Partnership
eligibility_notes: Charity-administered. Smaller grants but pairs well with statutory funds and has flexible timing.
